name: Kimi K3
license: Modified MIT License (precedent from the K2.x line; not yet formally published for K3)
archDisplay: 2.8T-parameter MoE, Stable LatentMoE framework activating 16 of 896 experts per token, Kimi Delta Attention (hybrid linear attention) with Attention Residuals, 1M-token context, always-on reasoning (single reasoning_effort=max tier at launch)
note: Moonshot's new flagship generation succeeding the K2.x line, launched July 16, 2026: 2.8T total parameters (vs. K2.6/K2.7's ~1T), a new Stable LatentMoE architecture (16-of-896 experts active per token, versus K2's 8 routed + 1 shared of 384) and Kimi Delta Attention, a 1M-token context window (up from K2's 256K), and a single always-on reasoning tier (no separate Instruct/Thinking split as with K2). Input pricing is $3.00/M tokens (cache-miss) with a 90%-discounted $0.30/M cache-hit rate, output $15.00/M. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: open weights are not yet published as of this research (2026-07-17, the day after launch) -- Moonshot has stated they will ship by July 27, 2026. The model is live now only via kimi.com, Kimi Work, Kimi Code, the Kimi API, and OpenRouter; no HuggingFace repo exists yet (huggingface.co/moonshotai still lists Kimi-K2.7-Code as the newest entry). The formal license text has also not been published; the value above reflects Moonshot's K2.x precedent (Modified MIT), not a confirmed K3 term -- re-verify once weights ship.

results.notes: GPQA-Diamond, official Kimi K3 launch blog knowledge/reasoning table. Independently re-verified against 3 convergent secondary reproductions of the official table (benchlm.ai, binaryverseai.com, mer.vin) -- all agree exactly on this figure.

Humanity's Last Exam, WITH tool use, official launch blog. A separate no-tools score of 43.5 was also published but is not recorded here to avoid conflating two different evaluation settings under one slug (matches this corpus's existing convention for other Kimi models, e.g. K2.5/K2.6 record only the tool-augmented HLE figure).
Terminal-Bench 2.1, official launch blog coding table. Independently re-verified via direct fetch confirming the same figure across 3 sources reproducing Moonshot's own table.
MCP Atlas, official launch blog agentic table.

BrowseComp, official launch blog agentic table. One secondary source rendered this as 90.4 -- within rounding/snapshot noise of the 91.2 figure independently confirmed across 3 other convergent reproductions of the official table.

GDPval-AA v2, independently measured by Artificial Analysis (raw Elo, matching this taxonomy's 0-2000-point scale as used by e.g. Gemini 3.1 Pro=1317, MiniMax M2.7=1495). A competing 1687 figure from a single non-AA community blog was discarded as a likely transcription error during adversarial re-verification (AA's own article and two independent convergent secondary reproductions all state 1668). NOTE: this is the only non-self-reported result in this entry -- included specifically to avoid the ranking resting entirely on vendor-chosen benchmarks, per this corpus's documented 'independent cross-checks are sparse' concern (see audit-data.ts).

Macro success, fourth overall and second open-weights model — clears OpenAI's flagship GPT-5.6 Sol (18%) but lands far behind GLM-5.2 (63%), inverting launch-week leaderboard verdicts. Wilson 95% CI [23, 41], mean shaped reward 0.799, total eval cost $187 (1.7x GLM-5.2's run for half the score). Per-slice exact successes /20: steward_7d 0, rulebook_7d 1, dedup 8, orienteering 9, gauntlet_7g 13. First-party serving lane worked through two documented serving-layer defects under the fail-closed gate, storm history disclosed per-row; 11% budget-exhaustion rate, all of it on the npp slice and none on macro rows.
Second of all eight models on the NPP anytime ladder, behind only Claude Fable 5 (0.471). Exact success 0/20, as preregistered for every model.

verificationNotes:
ROUND (2026-07-17): New model, added at explicit user request following Kimi K3's July 16, 2026 launch. Two-pass research: (1) initial broad research confirmed the model's existence, gathered specs and a wide benchmark set from official + independent sources; (2) dedicated adversarial re-verification pass independently re-fetched primary sources to resolve every discrepancy flagged by pass 1.

Resolved discrepancies: total params settled at 2.8T (a lone '2.7T' figure in one secondary source had no supporting citation anywhere and is treated as a transcription error). GDPval-AA v2 Elo settled at 1668 per Artificial Analysis's own article (a competing 1687 figure traced to a single non-AA community blog post, discarded).

Only 5 of the ~13 benchmark figures found are recorded here as results rows -- the rest (DeepSWE, FrontierSWE, Program Bench, SWE Marathon, Kimi Code Bench v2, AutomationBench, DeepSearchQA, MMMU-Pro, MathVision) are Moonshot in-house/proprietary suites or multimodal benchmarks with no matching taxonomy slug in this corpus, so they are omitted rather than mis-filed under an unrelated slug (matches this corpus's established citation-required, no-guessing convention). The 5 recorded here all map cleanly to existing taxonomy slugs already used by sibling Kimi K2.x entries.

Independent-source data explicitly NOT used: Vals.ai (vals.ai/models/kimi_kimi-k3) renders every individual per-benchmark score as a literal 0.0% -- a known broken/unrendered-scrape pattern already documented for other Kimi models in this corpus (see K2.7 Code's verificationNotes) -- only their aggregate Vals Index (74.70%, rank 2/38) is trustworthy from that source and it doesn't map to any taxonomy slug, so it's omitted too. Epoch AI has no Kimi K3 entry yet. Artificial Analysis's own model page (artificialanalysis.ai/models/kimi-k3) confirms an Intelligence Index of 57 and the GDPval-AA v2 Elo above, but AA's per-benchmark breakdown (Coding Index 76.2, Agentic Index 50.1, AA-Omniscience 46%/51% hallucination, AA-Briefcase Elo 1547) are AA's own composite/proprietary metrics, not standard-suite scores mappable to this corpus's taxonomy -- omitted for the same reason.

KNOWN LIMITATION: open weights are not yet public (promised July 27, 2026, 11 days after this entry was created), so no independent third party has been able to verify these vendor-reported numbers by running the actual weights -- all self-reported scores here should be treated with the same caution as any other pre-independent-verification vendor claim. Recommend re-checking Artificial Analysis, Vals.ai (hoping for a fixed scrape), and Epoch AI after the July 27 weight release for independent corroboration, and re-deriving the license field once Moonshot formally publishes K3's license text.

Also checked and explicitly ruled out: 'Kimi 3 Max' / 'Kimi 3 Swarm Max' variant claims appeared in exactly one low-trust secondary source with no corroboration from the official blog, OpenRouter, or platform docs -- not added, treated as unreliable reporting, possibly confused with a different vendor's product.

DATA-QUALITY NOTE (discovered while adding this entry, not fixed here): the gdpval-aa taxonomy entry declares a raw 0-2000-point Elo scale (matching Gemini 3.1 Pro=1317, MiniMax M2.7=1495, etc.), but Kimi K2.7 Code's own gdpval-aa row (34) and a few Qwen entries (23, 0, 39) appear to store a normalized 0-100 percentage instead -- a scale mismatch, not a new-data problem. Not corrected in this round since it is out of scope for a new-model-addition task; flagged for a future dedicated accuracy pass.

ROUND 3 (2026-07-18): Third and final research pass on the four remaining gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval), mandated to use source types NOT covered by rounds 1-2 (official/tracker sites; HELM/academic/GitHub-eval-logs/regional). All five newly-assigned source types were genuinely attempted:

1. WAYBACK MACHINE -- confirmed via archive.org's /wayback/available API that kimi.com/blog/kimi-k3 has exactly two crawled snapshots, 2026-07-16T22:25:41Z and 2026-07-17T13:28:46Z; querying every date from 2026-07-18 through 2026-07-29 still resolves to the same July-17 snapshot, proving NO later crawl exists yet -- there is no "before/after" window in the archive to test a "table quietly trimmed" hypothesis even in principle. artificialanalysis.ai/models/kimi-k3 likewise has only two same-window snapshots (2026-07-16T23:36:46Z, 2026-07-17T06:31:16Z). llm-stats.com/models/kimi-k3 has ZERO archived snapshots. Actual memento content retrieval was attempted 10+ times across two independent backoff sessions (up to 25s between retries) and was consistently blocked with HTTP 429 from web.archive.org's own serving infrastructure (the CDX-availability lookups on archive.org's root domain worked fine; only the web.archive.org memento-serving path itself throttled every attempt) -- a genuine, sustained attempt, not a cursory check. Given the snapshot dates found, this would very likely not have mattered anyway since no snapshot postdates the launch window.

2. CONFERENCE TALKS/KEYNOTES -- found one YouTube video ("Kimi K3: The 3 Trillion Open Source Coding King (Full Benchmarks and Test)", https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oFMflXi0PLQ) but fetch was blocked by a Google/YouTube bot-detection CAPTCHA redirect; no transcript retrievable. No official Moonshot recap keynote, developer-conference talk, or podcast transcript surfaced anywhere citing MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval for K3.

3. INVESTOR/PRESS MATERIALS -- Moonshot AI is privately held (Alibaba/Tencent/Meituan/HongShan/ZhenFund/IDG/5Y Capital-backed) and files no public earnings materials itself. Checked extensive press coverage of investors' market reaction (Alibaba -4%, Z.ai -30% in HK trading) via Bloomberg, CNBC, Fortune, Yahoo Finance, Axios -- all cover stock impact and qualitative/agentic-benchmark claims (Frontend Code Arena #1, GDPval-AA v2, AA-Briefcase) but none cites an MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figure.

4. GITHUB ISSUES/DISCUSSIONS -- used gh CLI to search every current official MoonshotAI repo (kimi-code, kimi-cli, kosong, kimi-agent-sdk, Kimi-K2, Kimi-K2.5, Kimi-Linear; no dedicated Kimi-K3 repo exists yet since weights aren't public) for issues/PRs mentioning K3 plus MMLU/AIME/HumanEval. Found only unrelated bug reports and one false-positive PR; no maintainer reply anywhere states a score for these four benchmarks.

5. THIRD-PARTY ANALYSIS SITES -- directly fetched Simon Willison's dedicated K3 post (simonwillison.net/2026/Jul/16/kimi-k3/): discusses the pelican-SVG test and AA's private Briefcase Elo, zero mention of MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. interconnects.ai has no dedicated K3 article yet (only prior K2-era pieces). SemiAnalysis coverage found is entirely about GB300/B300 hardware requirements for the 2.8T model, not benchmark scores. ChinaTalk had no dedicated K3 coverage surfaced. Also fetched BenchLM.ai's full model page, a GitHub fan-benchmark repo (47thtechcorner/Kimi-K3), and NxCode's coding-agent evaluation guide -- all three independently enumerate Moonshot's full ~20-benchmark launch table in detail (DeepSWE, FrontierSWE, ProgramBench, Kimi Code Bench v2, Terminal-Bench 2.1, SWE Marathon, PostTrainBench, MLS-Bench Lite, GDPval-AA v2, AA-Briefcase, JobBench, SpreadsheetBench 2, AutomationBench, BrowseComp, MCP Atlas, GPQA, HLE, MMMU-Pro, CharXiv, Zerobench, DeepSearchQA, Toolathlon-Verified, DECK-Bench, OfficeQA Pro) and each confirms MMLU, MATH-500, AIME, and HumanEval are simply absent -- these are secondary sources faithfully reproducing an already-incomplete primary table, not withholding data. A low-trust fan site (kimik3.xyz) had explicitly retracted a leaked benchmark table as "community speculation, removed," yielding no citable numbers either.

DISCARDED HALLUCINATION: a WebSearch auto-summary initially claimed "82.4% on MMLU-Pro" and "57.0% on AIME 2025" attributed to Artificial Analysis. Direct re-fetch of artificialanalysis.ai/models/kimi-k3 and artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/aime-2025 confirms neither figure actually appears there -- AA's page lists only its composite Intelligence Index (57, matching round 2's finding) and named-but-unscored component-benchmark labels, no MMLU/MMLU-Pro/AIME rows. The search-summarizer model appears to have conflated "Intelligence Index: 57" with an AIME score and sourced the MMLU-Pro figure from nowhere verifiable. Discarded per this corpus's no-invented/no-unverifiable-number rule. (Also note: even if genuine, MMLU-Pro is a distinct taxonomy slug ('mmlu-pro') from the 'mmlu' gap being chased here.)

CONCLUSION: three independent research rounds using three genuinely distinct sets of source types have now all failed to find mmlu, math (MATH-500), aime, or humaneval scores for Kimi K3. Best explanation remains Moonshot's launch table simply never including these four "saturated" legacy benchmarks (round 2's hypothesis), compounded by no independent party yet able to run standard eval harnesses since K3's weights are not public until 2026-07-27. Treating as a durable, confirmed absence; recommend one further check after the July 27 weight release when independent lm-evaluation-harness-style runs become possible for the first time.

ROUND 4 (2026-07-18): Chased three CURRENT-generation benchmark gaps -- livecodebench (LiveCodeBench), swe-bench (SWE-bench Verified), and mmmu (MMMU proper) -- using methods largely orthogonal to rounds 1-3 (which covered the legacy mmlu/math/aime/humaneval set).

MODALITY CHECK (mmmu): the corpus file's modalities include "vision" and capabilities.vision=true, so MMMU is architecturally applicable to K3 -- any absence must be evidentiary, not structural. Confirmed Moonshot does report MMMU-Pro (81.6% official-protocol / 83.4% with Python, per the launch blog, BenchLM, and llm-stats.com), but MMMU-Pro is a distinct taxonomy slug in this corpus ('mmmu-pro', normMin 15/normMax 88) from plain 'mmmu' (normMin 20/normMax 88) and per this corpus's own established convention (the mmlu/mmlu-pro distinction drawn in round 3) must not be conflated with it. No plain-MMMU score for K3 was found anywhere despite the modality check clearing it as applicable.

SOURCES CHECKED (6, exceeding the required 4-method minimum):
1. Official launch blog (kimi.com/blog/kimi-k3), re-fetched directly. Full enumerated benchmark list: coding (DeepSWE, Terminal-Bench 2.1, Program Bench, SWE Marathon, FrontierSWE, PostTrain Bench, MLS Bench Lite, KCB 2.0) and multimodal (PerceptionBench, MMMU-Pro, ZeroBench) -- no LiveCodeBench, no SWE-bench/SWE-bench Verified, no plain MMMU anywhere in the post.
2. Independent trackers, fetched directly. BenchLM.ai and NxCode's coding-agent evaluation guide both explicitly confirm LiveCodeBench, standard SWE-bench/SWE-bench Verified, and base MMMU are absent from Moonshot's table. Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/kimi-k3) shows only its own composite indices (Intelligence 57.11, Coding 76.24, Agentic 50.07) plus named-but-unscored component labels -- no LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench/MMMU rows. llm-stats.com/models/kimi-k3 (fetched live via browser since it's JS-rendered) enumerates ~31 tracked benchmarks for K3 (MathVision, DeepSearchQA, GPQA, CharXiv-R, BrowseComp, OmniDocBench, Terminal-Bench 2.1, BabyVision, MCP Atlas, MMMU-Pro x2, FrontierSWE, Program Bench, DECK-Bench, Toolathlon, Kimi Code Bench v2, DeepSWE, OfficeQA Pro, PerceptionBench, HLE, GDPval-AA, Job Bench, AA-Briefcase, WorldVQA, MLS-Bench Lite, SWE-Marathon, ZEROBench, APEX-Agents, PostTrainBench, SpreadsheetBench 2, AutomationBench) -- again zero LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench/MMMU entries. Vals.ai (vals.ai/models/kimi_kimi-k3) re-fetched directly: it does show a row literally labeled "SWE-bench" but the value renders as "0.0% +/- 1.11" -- reconfirming the exact broken-scrape pattern this corpus's round-2 verificationNotes already flagged for this source (every individual per-benchmark score there renders as a 0.0% placeholder); not usable as a citable number. (A WebSearch auto-summary separately claimed this same Vals.ai page showed "95.10% on SWE-bench Verified" and "91.27% on Vibe Code Bench" -- direct re-fetch disproved both figures, which do not appear anywhere on the actual page; discarded as a search-summarizer hallucination, the same failure mode as the round-3 MMLU-Pro/AIME hallucination already documented here.) LiveCodeBench's own official leaderboard (livecodebench.github.io) loaded live via browser: its current model roster tops out at O4-Mini/O3/Gemini-2.5-Pro/DeepSeek-R1-0528-era models with zero Kimi entries of any generation -- the tracker is simply too stale to carry a K3 row (or any Kimi row). SWE-bench's own official leaderboard (swebench.com) loaded live via browser: its Verified board is current through 2026-02-26 (Gemini 3 Pro, Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5-2, etc.) and DOES include "Kimi K2.5 (high reasoning)" at 70.80% resolved -- but no Kimi K3 row, consistent with K3 launching 2026-07-16 (after this board's last update) and its weights not yet being public (promised 2026-07-27) for the independent mini-SWE-agent harness runs this leaderboard requires.
3. Academic/arXiv -- no dedicated K3 technical report exists yet; secondary sources confirm K3's architecture citations point back to the October-2025 Kimi Linear paper (arXiv 2510.26692), not a K3-specific paper, so no academic baseline table citing K3 on these three benchmarks exists yet either.
4. GitHub eval-harness logs -- no MoonshotAI repo run-logs or third-party harness logs found citing K3 on LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench, or MMMU, consistent with round-3's gh-CLI sweep finding no dedicated Kimi-K3 repo (weights not public).
5. Sibling-model launch comparisons -- checked GPT-5.6 Sol and Claude Fable 5 vs. K3 comparison coverage (digitalapplied.com, modemguides.com, binaryverseai.com, the-decoder.com, officechai.com): all reproduce Moonshot's own proprietary-benchmark rows (FrontierSWE, SWE Marathon, Program Bench, DeepSWE, GDPval-AA v2, etc.) when comparing K3 to sibling-wave models; none cites a LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified, or MMMU figure for K3.
6. LMArena -- K3's LMArena presence is Elo-only (#1 Frontend Code Arena at 1679 Elo, 1486 overall text Elo); LMArena does not run LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench/MMMU as named benchmarks, so this method yields no citable number by design.

CONCLUSION: all three gaps are durable, well-corroborated absences rather than a search failure. Moonshot's K3 launch table substitutes proprietary/renamed benchmarks (DeepSWE, FrontierSWE, Program Bench, SWE Marathon, Kimi Code Bench v2, MMMU-Pro) for the standard LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified, and MMMU slugs this corpus tracks, and every independent tracker checked faithfully reproduces that same substituted set rather than filling the gap with its own measurement. Recommend re-checking swebench.com and livecodebench.github.io after the 2026-07-27 open-weight release, since both trackers' "official" entries require independently-run harnesses (mini-SWE-agent for SWE-bench, the LiveCodeBench harness for that benchmark) that cannot currently be executed against K3's not-yet-public weights.
